human_verification.notes: Reviewed the commercial-and-industrial-construction industrial-safety package for boundary quality, authority-source quality, hold-release logic, permit and inspection gating, repeat-incident handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to industrial construction safety support, hold-release and nonconformance logic, permit and inspection dependency awareness, and evidence-backed escalation rather than final authority.

Authority sources cover OSHA construction rules, OSHA recordkeeping, OSHA inspection evidence handling, multi-employer responsibility, public permitting workflow, public closeout workflow, and USACE industrial work-control expectations with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover contradictory evidence, release with open hold, repeat incidents, permit and site-readiness gaps, inspection and closeout blockers, safety-control bypass requests, code-interpretation refusal, policy conflicts, and tenant-specific industrial overlays.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/commercial-and-industrial-construction/industrial-safety-specialist/ to satisfy the issue's canonical overlay path while retaining the resolved construction-and-field-services semantic profile.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around permit approval, code interpretation, licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record authority, and release-bypass requests.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated industrial safety overlay where permit, inspection, engineering, licensed-trade, and regulator-facing authority remain external.

The specialist is intentionally collaboration-oriented: it knows when to hand work to project controls, permitting, inspection, safety leadership, superintendent, legal, engineering, and licensed-trade lanes.
Functionality coverage is complete and matches evaluation/results.json.
